title: malope malacoides subsp. hispida
intro: Malope malacoides subsp. hispida, also known as hispid jew's mallow or hispid mallow, is a subspecies of Malope malacoides, a type of flowering plant in the mallow family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which make it useful for treating skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Malope malacoides subsp. hispida include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as providing relief from symptoms of eczema and other skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to apply the plant's medicinal properties directly to the affected area.
